title: The Augmentation Trap: AI Productivity and the Cost of Cognitive Offloading
abstract: arXiv:2604.03501v5 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Experimental evidence suggests that AI tools raise worker productivity, but also that sustained use can erode the expertise on which those gains depend. To explore the consequences of this tradeoff, we develop a dynamic model in which a decision-maker chooses AI usage intensity for a worker over time, trading immediate productivity against the erosion of worker skill. We decompose the tool's productivity effect into two channels, one independent of worker expertise and one that scales with it. The model produces three main results. First, a decision-maker who fully anticipates skill erosion still rationally adopts AI when front-loaded gains outweigh long-run skill costs, lowering long-run productivity. The decomposition sorts deployments into five regimes by their long-run effect, separating beneficial from harmful adoption. Second, the tradeoff introduces the potential for misaligned incentives. translated_title: 人工智能陷阱：AI生产力与认知卸载的代价
translated_abstract: arXiv:2604.03501v5 公告类型：替换-交叉 摘要：实验证据表明，人工智能工具能提高工人生产力，但持续使用也可能侵蚀这些收益所依赖的专业知识。为了探索这一权衡的后果，我们开发了一个动态模型，其中决策者随时间推移为工人选择AI使用强度，在即时生产力与工人技能侵蚀之间进行权衡。我们将工具的生产力效应分解为两个渠道：一个独立于工人专业知识，另一个随专业知识增长而扩展。该模型得出三个主要结果。首先，完全预见到技能侵蚀的决策者仍会理性地采用AI，当前期收益超过长期技能成本时，这会降低长期生产力。该分解根据长期效应将部署分为五种状态，区分有益与有害的采用。其次，这种权衡引入了激励错位的可能性。当决策者未考虑长期成本时，可能过度依赖AI，导致工人技能下降。最后，模型揭示了“AI陷阱”：即使初始采用看似高效，持续使用可能使组织陷入技能退化与AI依赖的恶性循环，最终损害整体绩效。
tldr: 本文通过动态模型揭示，AI虽能提升短期生产力，但过度使用会侵蚀工人技能，导致长期生产力下降，并可能形成“AI陷阱”的恶性循环。
en_tdlr: This paper develops a dynamic model showing that while AI boosts short-term productivity, overuse erodes worker skills, leading to long-term productivity decline and a potential "AI trap" cycle.
